title: How to Install LeadConnector in GoHighLevel — WordPress Setup
description: Learn how to install LeadConnector on WordPress via GoHighLevel. Step-by-step guide covering automatic setup, plugin status, chat widgets, and OAuth reconnection.
html_content:
Installing LeadConnector on WordPress sites through GoHighLevel is one of the fastest ways to supercharge your lead management and customer communication. If you're running an agency or managing multiple client WordPress sites, you need a streamlined way to integrate chat, SMS, email, and automation without touching code or juggling multiple plugins.

In this guide, I'll walk you through the exact process of setting up LeadConnector in WordPress via GoHighLevel Hosting—including automatic installation, plugin management, and OAuth reconnection methods. By the end, you'll understand how to leverage this powerful integration to deliver better results for your clients.

Ready to see what's possible? Start a free 30-day GoHighLevel trial to test this setup yourself.

📋 In This Guide

  • Understanding LeadConnector and GoHighLevel WordPress Hosting
  • How Automatic LeadConnector Installation Works
  • Checking Plugin Status and Health Indicators
  • Enabling and Managing Chat Widgets in WordPress
  • Reconnecting LeadConnector Using OAuth
  • Key Benefits for Agencies and Businesses

Understanding LeadConnector and GoHighLevel WordPress Hosting

LeadConnector is GoHighLevel's native WordPress plugin—a bridge that connects your entire CRM, marketing automation, and communication suite directly to WordPress. Instead of manually integrating SMS gateways, email platforms, and chat tools, LeadConnector handles it all in one place.

When you host a WordPress site through GoHighLevel's hosting infrastructure, the platform automatically recognizes this and streamlines the LeadConnector setup process. This is a game-changer for agencies because it means your clients get a fully integrated experience without technical headaches.

The core advantage: LeadConnector syncs your GoHighLevel contacts, pipelines, and automation directly to WordPress. When a visitor submits a form, adds to cart, or engages with a chat widget, that data flows seamlessly into GoHighLevel's CRM. No webhooks to configure. No API keys to manage manually. It just works.

How Automatic LeadConnector Installation Works

When you create a new WordPress site through GoHighLevel Hosting, the platform automatically installs the LeadConnector plugin. You don't need to manually download files, upload via FTP, or activate anything through the WordPress dashboard.

Here's the automatic installation flow:

1. Create or connect a WordPress site in GoHighLevel
Navigate to your GoHighLevel account and go to Websites > WordPress. Either create a new site or connect an existing one through GoHighLevel Hosting.

2. GoHighLevel provisions the LeadConnector plugin
Behind the scenes, the platform automatically pushes the LeadConnector plugin to your WordPress environment and sets initial connection parameters.

3. Plugin authenticates with your account
The plugin uses OAuth to authenticate with your GoHighLevel account, ensuring secure data flow between your WordPress site and your CRM.

4. Status synchronizes in your dashboard
You'll see real-time connection status in your GoHighLevel Websites section. A green indicator means everything is connected and ready to use.

💡 Pro Tip

If you're migrating an existing WordPress site into GoHighLevel Hosting, the platform will detect any existing LeadConnector installation and automatically upgrade it to the latest version. No plugin conflicts, no manual cleanup required.

Checking Plugin Status and Health Indicators

Not all plugin connections are created equal. GoHighLevel provides clear status indicators so you know whether LeadConnector is functioning properly.

Green Status (Connected & Active)
Your LeadConnector plugin is installed, authenticated, and actively syncing data with GoHighLevel. Forms, chat widgets, and automation are all live. This is what you want to see.

Yellow Status (Connected but Limited)
The plugin is installed and authenticated, but certain features may be disabled or limited. This typically occurs when your subscription plan doesn't include advanced features like phone integration or custom chat branding. Check your plan settings to understand what's available.

Red Status (Disconnected)
The plugin exists on your WordPress site but is not authenticated with your GoHighLevel account. Data won't sync, chat widgets won't appear, and form submissions may not reach your CRM. See the OAuth reconnection section below to fix this.

To check your plugin status:

1. Log into GoHighLevel
2. Navigate to Websites > WordPress Sites
3. Find your site and check the connection indicator (colored circle)
4. Click the site to see detailed health metrics and last sync timestamp

Enabling and Managing Chat Widgets in WordPress

Once LeadConnector is installed and connected, enabling chat on your WordPress site is straightforward. Chat widgets are one of the most powerful lead-capture tools in GoHighLevel, and they integrate natively through the plugin.

Step 1: Access your WordPress site settings in GoHighLevel
In your GoHighLevel account, go to Websites and select your WordPress site.

Step 2: Find LeadConnector plugin settings
Look for a "LeadConnector" or "Integrations" tab within the site settings. Click to expand plugin options.

Step 3: Enable chat widget
Toggle the chat widget option to ON. You'll see a sub-menu appear with configuration options like widget position, color scheme, greeting message, and assign team members.

Step 4: Customize widget appearance
Match the chat widget to your brand. Choose colors that complement your site, write a welcoming greeting, and decide if you want avatars or team member names displayed.

Step 5: Assign chat responders
Select which GoHighLevel users or team members will handle incoming chats. You can assign by location, pipeline, or tags.

Step 6: Test and deploy
Save your settings and visit your WordPress site's front end. You should see the chat widget in your selected position. Send a test message to confirm it's reaching your GoHighLevel inbox.

Reconnecting LeadConnector Using OAuth

If your LeadConnector plugin shows a red status or displays a "Connection Lost" message, the OAuth authentication has been interrupted. This happens occasionally due to password resets, account security changes, or token expiration. The good news: reconnecting takes just a few minutes.

What is OAuth and why does it matter?
OAuth is a secure authentication standard that allows LeadConnector to communicate with GoHighLevel without storing your password. Instead, you grant permission once, and the plugin receives a token to access only the data it needs.

Step-by-step OAuth reconnection:

1. Navigate to your disconnected site in GoHighLevel
Go to Websites > WordPress Sites and click on the site showing red status.

2. Click "Reconnect" or "Reauthorize"
Look for a prominent button near the connection status. Click it to initiate the OAuth flow.

3. You'll be redirected to a GoHighLevel login screen
Log in with the account that owns the WordPress site. This verifies your identity.

4. Grant permission for LeadConnector
A permission dialog appears asking if you authorize the LeadConnector plugin to access your CRM data. Click "Authorize" or "Allow."

5. Confirmation and automatic redirect
You'll see a "Successfully Connected" message and be redirected back to your GoHighLevel dashboard. The plugin status should immediately turn green.

6. Verify data sync
Wait 30 seconds, then refresh your WordPress site's front end. Chat widgets should appear, forms should submit to your CRM, and your site should feel fully connected again.

What if OAuth reconnection fails?
Clear your browser cache and cookies, then try again. If the problem persists, check that your GoHighLevel account hasn't been suspended or that you're using the correct login credentials. Contact GoHighLevel support if you're still unable to reconnect.

Key Benefits of LeadConnector for Agencies

Understanding why LeadConnector matters will help you pitch it to clients and maximize its use in your own business.

Unified lead capture across all channels
Every form, chat, SMS, and email interaction flows into one CRM. Your team sees a complete customer timeline instead of scattered data across platforms.

No technical barriers for clients
Agencies can install LeadConnector on client WordPress sites without the client needing developer access or understanding API integration. They just get results.

Automation that actually works
Create workflows that trigger on WordPress form submissions, cart abandonment, or chat engagement. Segment leads, send follow-up emails, assign to sales reps—all automatically.

Real-time lead routing
Assign incoming chats, calls, and leads to team members based on availability, expertise, or location. No manual assignment. No lost leads.

White-label capability for agencies
Agencies can rebrand chat widgets, email notifications, and landing pages. Your clients see your brand, not GoHighLevel's.

For agencies managing 10+ client sites, LeadConnector eliminates the need for separate chat platforms, form plugins, and CRM connectors. That's significant time saved and fewer points of failure.
